q: Where does the oxygen released during photosynthesis come from?
options: From the splitting of CO2 molecules during the Calvin cycle
From O2 dissolved in the stroma of the chloroplast
From the photolysis (light-driven splitting) of water molecules in the thylakoid membrane
From the oxidation of glucose intermediates in the light-independent reactions
explanation: Water is split by light energy (photolysis) in the thylakoid; the O2 released is a by-product of this reaction, not from CO2 splitting.
